title: Syncalathium porphyreum
taxonomy: Syncalathium porphyreum belongs to the family Asteraceae. It is a genus of flowering plants in the family Asteraceae and is native to the mountains of Central Asia. Syncalathium porphyreum is a species within this genus. The classification of this plant is further divided into its binomial nomenclature, Syncalathium porphyreum. This classification is used to identify and distinguish Syncalathium porphyreum from other species within the same genus and family.
uses: Syncalathium porphyreum is reportedly used in traditional medicine in Central Asia for its various health benefits. It is believed to have antimicrobial and antifungal properties, making it effective in treating infections. The plant's roots are sometimes used to treat digestive issues and inflammation. However, more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption. Its medicinal uses may also extend to treating colds and other respiratory issues.
benefits: Syncalathium porphyreum has not been extensively researched for its health benefits. However, some studies suggest that the plant's extracts may have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. These potential properties could be beneficial in protecting against oxidative stress, reducing inflammation, and preventing infections. Further research is needed to confirm the plant's health benefits and potential uses. Additional studies may also provide insights into its safety and efficacy as a medicinal agent.

constituents: Syncalathium porphyreum contains sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids as its active constituents.
